command: $gw='192.0.2.255'; Test-NetConnection -ComputerName $gw -Port 7 -InformationLevel Quiet | Out-Null; $mac='AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF'; $mb=$mac.Split('-') | ForEach-Object {[byte]('0x'+$_)}; $pkt = @([byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF); 1..16 | ForEach-Object { $pkt += $mb }; $udp = New-Object System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient; $udp.EnableBroadcast = $true; $udp.Send($pkt, $pkt.Length, $gw, 7) | Out-Null
explanation: Sends a WOL-formatted magic packet to a directed broadcast address on UDP/7 to trigger device wake. Detection: flag directed broadcasts carrying WOL signatures; monitor for WOL packets outside authorized windows or VLANs.
rationale_steps: Validate reachability to the directed broadcast on UDP/7 using Test-NetConnection.
Build the correct WOL magic packet structure.
Transmit the packet to the subnet broadcast to wake the device.
Defenders: alert on WOL packets traversing routing boundaries or outside maintenance windows.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: PowerShell syntax is well-formed with balanced quoting, valid pipelines, and correct method/property usage.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It correctly builds and sends a WOL magic packet over UDP broadcast aligning with Traffic Signaling, though the TEST-NET address and use of port 7 may reduce real-world effectiveness.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced quotes, proper semicolons as separators, valid array construction, correct .NET socket API usage, and no syntax errors.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly constructs a Wake-on-LAN magic packet (6x 0xFF followed by 16 repetitions of the target MAC) and sends it via UDP broadcast, which aligns with T1205 traffic signaling by embedding a trigger signal in legitimate-looking network traffic; the minor gap is that WoL typically uses port 9 rather than port 7, and the preliminary Test-NetConnection check on port 7 is slightly unusual but not operationally implausible.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell syntax is perfectly formed, uses correct object methods, and handles byte array construction correctly.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately constructs a standard Wake-on-LAN magic packet and sends it to a broadcast address, aligning perfectly with the intent of signaling a host.
